awaitingDates.income: {count, plural, one {1 income source} other {# income sources}}
awaitingDates.suffix: awaiting pay dates — not yet in this month's ledger.

landing.badge: AI financial coach for Canadian families

hero.title: Your family finances, finally clear.
hero.subtitle: Fill in our simple template — or enter your numbers by hand. Get a complete financial plan in 5 minutes. Know exactly what to do this month.
hero.ctaNote: Not open yet — check back soon.
hero.socialProof: Built for Canadian families · RRSP, RESP, TFSA aware · English & Français

problem.title: The real reason your credit line keeps growing
problem.subtitle: It's not the groceries. It's not the coffee. It's the $8,000 in predictable annual expenses that arrive with no account waiting for them.

taxes.title: Property taxes
taxes.description: Two installments, March and June. Every year. Still a surprise.

registration.title: Car registration & insurance
registration.description: Predictable. Annual. Straight onto the credit line.

school.title: Back to school
school.description: September hits the same way every year. $800 per kid, no plan.

income_tax.title: Income tax balance
income_tax.description: Quebec residents with Ontario employers owe provincial tax every April. Every year.

howItWorks.title: From spreadsheet to financial plan in 5 minutes

upload.title: Upload your budget
upload.description: Fill in our simple template — or enter your numbers by hand. Phare builds your complete financial picture from it.

plan.title: Get your plan
plan.description: Phare checks your numbers, asks only what's missing — real pay dates, who earns what — and gives you a clear plan you can trust.

review.title: Monthly review
review.description: On the 1st of each month, Phare tells you exactly what happened and what to do next. Like a letter from your financial advisor.

features.title: Everything your family needs. Nothing it doesn't.

familyBudgets.title: Family member budgets
familyBudgets.description: Individual spending limits within a shared household budget. Both spouses see the same dashboard. Alerts at 80% and 100%.

sinkingFunds.title: Sinking funds
sinkingFunds.description: Annual expenses provisioned monthly so they never hit the credit line again. Property taxes, car registration, back to school — all covered.

goalTracking.title: Goal tracking
goalTracking.description: Disney 2028. Brazil 2029. See exactly whether you're on track and by how much.

monthlyReview.title: Monthly review
monthlyReview.description: AI-generated, plain language, specific numbers. One clear recommendation. Delivered on the 1st of each month.

pricing.title: A financial advisor charges $250/hour
pricing.subtitle: Phare costs less than a family dinner out.

free.name: Phare Lighthouse
free.ctaNote: Not open yet — check back soon.

pro.name: Phare Lighthouse Pro
pro.founding: Founding families: $15/month, locked forever — first 50 families.

finalCta.title: Your family deserves a financial plan.
finalCta.subtitle: Not another spreadsheet. Not another app that tracks what you already spent. A plan that tells you what to do next.
finalCta.ctaNote: Not open yet — check back soon.

upload.title: Upload your financial data
upload.subtitle: Download the Phare template and fill in your numbers, or enter them manually.
upload.dropzone: Drag and drop your filled-in template here, or click to browse

mismatch.wrongFile: This doesn't look like the Phare template. Download it below and fill in your numbers.
mismatch.outdatedTemplate: This file doesn't match the current Phare template — download the latest version and copy your numbers in.

noFile.title: Get the template
noFile.description: Download it, fill in your numbers, and upload it below. Takes about 10 minutes.

memberConfirm.title: Who's who?
memberConfirm.subtitle: A few names in your file don't match anyone in your household yet.
memberConfirm.question: Your file mentions {name} — part of your household?
memberConfirm.declinedNote: Skipped — this income will be assigned to you, and flagged so you can fix it later.
memberConfirm.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

accounts.title: Your accounts
accounts.subtitle: How many credit cards do you want to track? Your chequing account is set up automatically.

confirm.title: Here's what we found
confirm.subtitle: Review and confirm before we build your plan.
confirm.confirmBtn: This looks right — build my plan

plan.goalsEmpty: You haven't set any goals yet — add them on the Goals page.
plan.sinkingFundsEmpty: You haven't set up any sinking funds yet — you can add them anytime as annual expenses come up.
plan.notAchievable: {statedDate} needs {amount}/mo — at your current capacity, {estimatedDate} is realistic.
plan.reviewError: We couldn't generate your review just now. Please try again.
plan.reviewUnavailableForSave: Your first review couldn't be generated, but your plan is saved. You can regenerate a review from your dashboard.

replaceConfirm.title: Replace data from your previous import?
replaceConfirm.body: {count, plural, one {This will replace 1 recurring item and its transactions from your last import with this new plan.} other {This will replace # recurring items and their transactions from your last import with this new plan.}}
replaceConfirm.legacy: {count, plural, one {We also found 1 older recurring item from before we tracked imports. It won't be touched — review it on the Recurring page if it looks out of date.} other {We also found # older recurring items from before we tracked imports. They won't be touched — review them on the Recurring page if they look out of date.}}
replaceConfirm.accountsReuseTitle: Accounts that will be kept and reused (matched by name):
replaceConfirm.accountsDeleteTitle: Accounts that will be removed and recreated:
replaceConfirm.accountsPreserveTitle: Accounts that will be kept as-is:

reason.has_envelope_budget: it has category budgets set up
reason.has_monthly_goal: it has a monthly goal set

plan.unmatchedMembers: {count, plural, one {1 income source's Member name didn't match anyone in your household — it was assigned to you. Check it on the Recurring page.} other {# income sources' Member names didn't match anyone in your household — they were assigned to you. Check them on the Recurring page.}}
plan.unmatchedMemberItem: “{name}” didn't match anyone — assigned to you.
plan.needsDateFlag: set a payment date to see this month

anchorStep.title: When do these get paid?
anchorStep.subtitle: Add a real date for each of these so it shows up on the right days — a paycheque you receive, or a bill you pay. You can skip any of these and set them later on the Recurring page.
anchorStep.fallbackFlag: “{name}” didn’t match anyone — assigned to you.
anchorStep.nextPayDateWindow: Must be within the next {days} days — that's what makes it the *next* payday.
anchorStep.shortMonthNote: 30th or 31st? In shorter months (like February), it lands on the last day instead.

batchResult.allSaved: {saved, plural, one {1 date saved.} other {# dates saved.}}
batchResult.partial: {saved, plural, one {1 date saved} other {# dates saved}}, {failed, plural, one {1 needs attention above} other {# need attention above}}.

anchorStep.skipNote: Anything left blank stays flagged as "needs a pay date" — you can add it later on the Recurring page.

confirmSkip.income: {count, plural, one {1 income source} other {# income sources}}
confirmSkip.message: {parts} won't appear in your month view until they have dates. Continue anyway?

error.tooFar: That's more than {days} days away — the next payday should be sooner than that.
error.same: The two paydays must be different days.
error.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.

form.title: Enter your numbers
form.subtitle: Add your income and expenses below — Phare builds your financial picture from it.
form.incomeHint: Enter what lands in your bank account each pay, then pick the frequency. We compute the monthly figure — no math needed.
form.expenseHint: Enter what you pay each time, then pick how often. We compute the monthly figure — no math needed.
form.combinedIncomeHint: Annual estimate — used to verify your income entries add up correctly

plausibility.title: Double-check your income
plausibility.skippedIncomeRows: {count, plural, one {1 income row could not be read — check the Frequency column spelling (accepted: weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly, monthly).} other {# income rows could not be read — check the Frequency column spelling (accepted: weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly, monthly).}}
plausibility.skippedGoalDateRows: {count, plural, one {1 goal's target date could not be read — check the Target date column (accepted: a real date, or text like "September 2026").} other {# goals' target dates could not be read — check the Target date column (accepted: a real date, or text like "September 2026").}}
plausibility.incomeVsStated: You mentioned a combined income around {stated}/year, but the entries add up to about {computed}/year — does that look right?
plausibility.deficitNotFinanced: Your plan shows a {deficit}/month shortfall with no credit line or debt payment to explain it. If your income is understated, correct it now — a deficit on paper needs to be financed by something real.
plausibility.confirm: These numbers are right — continue anyway

auth.signinSubtitle: Sign in to see your family's plan.
auth.signupSubtitle: Your financial plan, saved and waiting every month.
auth.checkEmail: Check your email to confirm your account.

dashboard.noPlan: You don't have a plan yet.
dashboard.noPlanCta: Upload your budget to get started

cards.title: Card Envelopes
cards.noCards: No credit cards set up yet.
cards.noCardsHint: Credit cards are added when you set up your household.

decision.noEnvelope: No envelope set — add categories below to track spending by category.

editor.title: Edit envelope
editor.sumWarning: Category totals ({sum}) exceed the card goal ({goal}). The card goal takes priority — adjust if needed.
editor.copyNothingToCopy: No envelope was saved for the previous month.

grid.title: 12-month plan
grid.empty: Set up envelope categories to see your 12-month plan.

header.noDip: No income scheduled ahead in this view — balance shown through {date}.

list.empty: Nothing on the books this month.
list.balancesBegin: Balances begin {date} — the date of your first anchor.

nav.outOfRange: Outside the range Phare tracks for this account

noAnchor.title: To show your timeline, Phare needs one real number: your chequing balance on a date you know.

goals.title: Goals
goals.noGoals: No savings goals yet. Create your first one below.

typeDesc.tfsa: Tax-Free Savings Account — contributions grow tax-free
typeDesc.rrsp: Registered Retirement Savings Plan — contributions reduce taxable income

recurring.title: Recurring
recurring.subtitle: Your fixed income and expenses — set once, they fill every month.

list.empty: No recurring lines yet. Add your mortgage, salary, loans above.
list.confirmBody: Future entries will be removed. Past entries stay as history.

reconcile.title: Reconciliation Audit
reconcile.subtitle: Debugging instrument — every money number for the month, traced to the ledger. Two independently-derived nets must agree; a delta is the bug.
reconcile.mismatch: Mismatch — the two derivation paths disagree

dualNet.title: Dual-Net Comparison
dualNet.footnote: Path 1 uses computeMonthTotals (bucket logic). Path 2 sums chequing rows directly by sign. Equal → ledger is clean.

household.title: Household Members
household.subtitle: Manage who has access to your household's financial data.
household.roleMemberDesc: Can view and manage all financial data. Cannot manage members.
household.successBody: A set-password email has been sent to {email}. They'll click the link to set their password and sign in.
household.resentBody: A new set-password email has been sent to {email}. The previous link may have expired.
household.attachedBody: {name} was already part of your household — we attached this invite to that existing record instead of creating a duplicate. A set-password email has been sent to {email}.

disambiguation.prompt: "{name}" matches more than one existing household member. Is this one of them, or a new person?
disambiguation.attachTo: This is {name} — attach the invite to them
disambiguation.createNew: This is someone new — create a new person

household.notOwner: Only the household owner can manage members.
